title: Reading and Use of English – Part 3
description: For questions 17–24, read the text below. Use the word given in CAPITALS at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: Jazz is a genre that (0) ORIGINALLY emerged from the African American communities of New Orleans. It is (17) for its swing and blue notes, complex chords, call and response vocals, and improvisation. While some feared the (18) of traditional structures, jazz actually (19) the horizons of modern music. Talented (20) from diverse backgrounds contributed to its growth, (21) altering the cultural landscape. Over the decades, many (22) have appeared, ranging from bebop to fusion. This flexibility (23) the genre to stay relevant in a fast-paced world. The (24) of jazz is a testament to its profound emotional depth and its ability to reflect the human experience across different eras and continents. It remains a powerful medium of artistic expression that continues to inspire and challenge artists and listeners alike around the globe.

example.text: Jazz is a genre that (0) ORIGINALLY (ORIGIN) emerged from the African American communities of New Orleans.
example.explanation: Adverb required to modify the verb 'emerged'; ORIGIN → ORIGINALLY.

questions.explanation: Adjective required to describe the genre; CHARACTER → CHARACTERISTIC.
Noun required with a negative prefix to indicate loss; APPEAR → DISAPPEARANCE.
Verb in past tense required; BROAD → BROADENED.
Plural noun for people required; MUSIC → MUSICIANS.
Adverb required to modify the verb 'altering'; SIGNIFY → SIGNIFICANTLY.
Verb in present simple third person required; ABLE → ENABLES.
Noun required following the definite article; ENDURE → ENDURANCE.
